page: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-192-metal is a compute-optimized bare metal server featuring 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8481C processors. The architecture is x86_64, with dedicated CPU allocation running at a base speed of 4.0 GHz. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core. The server does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, focusing its resource density entirely on raw CPU and memory performance.

Benchmark data shows top-tier performance in multi-core CPU execution, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 160623.56 and a PassMark CPU score of 123975.15. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, reaching 13143.3 GB/sec for cached small block reads and 466.69 GB/sec for uncached large block reads. The server also demonstrates top-tier capabilities in database operations, achieving 36276.2 in PassMark database benchmarks and 182.38 million operations per second in Redis SET operations.

The Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations. These hardware features translate to strong performance in LLM inference, processing 254.3 tokens per second for medium 7B models and 35.22 tokens per second for large 70B models. The server is suited for high-throughput web serving, software compilation, database management, and CPU-based machine learning workloads.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-192-metal is a compute-optimized bare metal server featuring 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8481C processors. Operating at 4.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 768.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core. The server lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 160623.56 and a PassMark CPU score of 123975.15. It excels in memory bandwidth, database operations, and Redis throughput, achieving 182.38 million operations per second. With support for Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512, this bare metal instance is highly suited for demanding workloads such as large-scale database operations, software compilation, high-throughput web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-192-metal is a bare metal server with 192 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and Intel Xeon processors, delivering top-tier multi-core CPU and database performance.

tagline: A high-core bare metal server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and DDR5 memory for intensive compute and database workloads.
bullet_points: 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8481C processors at 4.0 GHz.
768.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory with 4.0 GB per core.
Top-tier multi-core performance with a stress-ng score of 160623.56.
Excellent database throughput, reaching 182.38 million Redis SET operations per second.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated CPU-based machine learning inference.
Best suited for large-scale databases, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
